title: PM Cram practice question
description: A single practice/exam question. The app assembles the on-screen options at runtime from `correct` + `distractors`, so options are never hard-coded as A/B/C/D and never appear in a fixed order.

id.description: Globally unique, stable, kebab-case. Used to dedupe across files.

eco_task.description: ECO task / enabler this question maps to (free text).

type.description: single = choose one; multi = choose N.

select.description: How many options the user must choose. Must equal the number of entries in `correct`.

present.description: Total options to render = correct shown + distractors sampled. Must be > select and <= (correct.length + distractors.length).

prompts.description: One or more equivalent wordings of the same question. The app picks one at random so repeat exposures feel fresh.

correct.description: The correct option(s). For `multi`, length should equal `select`.

why.description: Per-option explanation of why it is correct (shown on review).

distractors.description: Pool of wrong answers. Provide MORE than needed — the app samples `present - select` of them each time so the wrong set varies.

trap.description: Why this option is wrong / the misconception it represents (shown on review).

contributor.description: Optional GitHub handle of the contributor.
